texts.full_text: for years. This time I caught something that had slipped by me before – I may have noticed it, but the import escaped me. Writing with wisdom and insight, Fr. Dajczer addresses many aspects of the spiritual life, and devotes an entire section to The Actualization of Faith. In it he has a subsection on The Role of Feelings, wherein he makes an amazing statement, which I'll quote: “It couldbe said that our feelings are in some sense more real than we. We experience them and observe their changes within us; but they do not exist because of or through the fact that 'I' am experiencing this feeling. Rather, the reverse is true: it seems as if, at a given moment of time and under certain conditions – even though these may be difficult to determine – we are born in and with our feelings.” Wow. This was one statement I didn't want my mind going over too quickly. He's not talking about the emotions we have from day-to-day, the ones that come on us like waves, sometimes gently washing up on shore as they roll past; other times crashing down upon

texts.full_text: ed specimens were embedded in Epon 812 and sectioned for transmission electron microscopy (TEM). The cardiac endothelium, when fixed immediately in buffered aldehydes, presents an essentially invariable surface throughout the interior of the heart. The predominant nuclear bulges and attenuated peripheral plasmatemma are consistently smooth, with occasional marginal ruffles, scatted microvilli, and rare, small, circular apertures that likely represent interendothelial junctions or fenestrae. In areas of increased hemodynamic stress, such as near valve cusps and at branch points of major

texts.preview_tail: u are trying to access an element with key "numeric", which PHP interprets differently when preceded by ->, this should solve syntax error issues and allow proper value retrieval from SimpleXMLElement
texts.full_text: having trouble getting the value out of it. echo $object->0; //returns Parse error: syntax error, unexpected T_LNUMBER, expecting T_STRING or T_VARIABLE or '{' or '$' SimpleXMLElement Object ( [0:public] => 15810 ) Any ideas on how I can get that value? Update I realize that this is an odd error... I'm using the ebay API to get this value. Even when I do: $zero = 0; $print_r($ruleXml->HourlyUsage->$zero); It still shows the same SimpleXMLElement Object ( [0:public] => 15810 ) I tried {0} as well Here's the output of what I'm working with.... [1] => SimpleXMLElement Object ( [CallName:public] => AddItem [CountsTowardAggregate:public] => false [DailyHardLimit:public] => 100000 [DailySoftLimit:public] => 100000 [DailyUsage:public] => 0 [HourlyHardLimit:public] => 100000 [HourlySoftLimit:public] => 100000 [HourlyUsage:public] => 0 [Period:public] => -1 [PeriodicHardLimit:public] => 0 [PeriodicSoftLimit:public] => 0 A possible solution to your issue is casting the numeric property as a string before accessing it. Since you are trying to access an element with key "numeric", which PHP interprets differently when preceded by ->, this should solve syntax error issues and allow proper value retrieval from SimpleXMLElement
